---
applyTo: "src/app/api/**/*.ts,openapi-ts.config.ts"
---

# Generated API Instructions

## Treat src/app/api As Generated Output

- Files under [src/app/api](../../src/app/api) are generated by `@hey-api/openapi-ts`, not hand-maintained source.
- Generated files currently include banners such as `This file is auto-generated by @hey-api/openapi-ts`. Treat those files as replaceable artifacts.
- Do not make manual edits in [src/app/api](../../src/app/api) unless the task is explicitly about generator output and there is no viable regeneration path.

## Preferred Change Path

- If API models, endpoints, enum output, or date transformation behavior need to change, first update the OpenAPI source on the backend or adjust [openapi-ts.config.ts](../../openapi-ts.config.ts).
- Then regenerate the client with `npm run openapi-ts`.
- Avoid patching generated files to fix symptoms that actually come from the Swagger document or generator config.

## What The Current Generator Does

- Generator config lives in [openapi-ts.config.ts](../../openapi-ts.config.ts).
- The current input source is `http://localhost:5600/swagger/v1/swagger.json`.
- Output is written to [src/app/api](../../src/app/api).
- The config enables:
  - Type generation through `@hey-api/typescript`
  - date types and response transformers
  - JavaScript enum output
  - preserved definition casing

## Lint And Verification Caveat

- The lint config ignores [src/app/api](../../src/app/api) via [eslint.config.js](../../eslint.config.js), so lint will not protect you from mistakes inside generated files.
- If generated output changes, validate with the smallest useful command after regeneration, typically `npm run build` or a targeted test path that exercises the affected API consumer.

## Import And Usage Guidance

- Prefer importing generated types from the barrel in [src/app/api/index.ts](../../src/app/api/index.ts) unless an existing local pattern intentionally uses deeper imports.
- Keep hand-written service logic, caching, and UI mapping outside the generated folder. The generated client should stay a thin artifact layer.

## When Manual Edits Might Be Acceptable

- Only hand-edit generated files as a short-term last resort when:
  - the user explicitly asks for it, or
  - regeneration is impossible in the current environment and the change must unblock work immediately
- If you do that, call out clearly that the edit will be overwritten on the next generation run and should be folded back into the OpenAPI source or generator config afterward.
